known_false_positives: partitioned inference fleet misread as training capacity
features.missingness_behavior: Missing inventory prevents strong capacity and no-run claims; do not infer absence of capacity from missing inventory.
features.name: Accelerator count
features.normalization: Normalize by hardware generation and sustained training capacity table; preserve raw counts separately.

range.notes: Count alone is capacity

trust_requirements: cross-check against scheduler/GRES and physical/change records

known_false_positives: partitioned inference fleet misread as training capacity
features.missingness_behavior: Missing inventory prevents strong capacity and no-run claims; do not infer absence of capacity from missing inventory.
features.name: H100-equivalent or sustained training compute
features.normalization: Normalize by hardware generation and sustained training capacity table; preserve raw counts separately.

range.notes: Upper bound depends on installed hardware and normalization table.

trust_requirements: cross-check against scheduler/GRES and physical/change records

known_false_positives: partitioned inference fleet misread as training capacity
features.missingness_behavior: Missing inventory prevents strong capacity and no-run claims; do not infer absence of capacity from missing inventory.
features.name: Homogeneous high-end accelerator fraction
features.normalization: Normalize by hardware generation and sustained training capacity table; preserve raw counts separately.

range.notes: 0 means none of the relevant fleet is homogeneous high-end; 1 means all relevant devices match the high-end target class.

trust_requirements: cross-check against scheduler/GRES and physical/change records

known_false_positives: partitioned inference fleet misread as training capacity
features.missingness_behavior: Missing inventory prevents strong capacity and no-run claims; do not infer absence of capacity from missing inventory.
features.name: Low-latency topology footprint
features.normalization: Normalize by hardware generation and sustained training capacity table; preserve raw counts separately.

range.notes: Use 0 when no accelerator topology domain is present; otherwise domain size or graph-derived footprint.

trust_requirements: cross-check against scheduler/GRES and physical/change records

known_false_positives: partitioned inference fleet misread as training capacity
features.missingness_behavior: Missing inventory prevents strong capacity and no-run claims; do not infer absence of capacity from missing inventory.
features.name: MIG/vGPU/MPS partitioning fraction
features.normalization: Normalize by hardware generation and sustained training capacity table; preserve raw counts separately.

range.notes: 0 means unpartitioned; 1 means all relevant accelerators are partitioned or shared.

trust_requirements: cross-check against scheduler/GRES and physical/change records

known_false_positives: partitioned inference fleet misread as training capacity
features.missingness_behavior: Missing inventory prevents strong capacity and no-run claims; do not infer absence of capacity from missing inventory.
features.name: Inventory delta / hardware swap rate
features.normalization: Normalize by hardware generation and sustained training capacity table; preserve raw counts separately.

range.notes: Counts additions

trust_requirements: cross-check against scheduler/GRES and physical/change records

O10.category: Primary semantic / attribution signal, when accessible

features.missingness_behavior: Runtime metadata may be absent under tenant isolation; absence weakens attribution but does not rule out training.
features.name: Distributed WORLD_SIZE / rank count
features.normalization: Hash sensitive identifiers, map framework classes coarsely, and align runtime records with scheduler/fabric windows.

range.notes: Use 0 only for no observed distributed runtime; PyTorch WORLD_SIZE itself is positive when present.

features.missingness_behavior: Runtime metadata may be absent under tenant isolation; absence weakens attribution but does not rule out training.
features.name: Runtime framework class
features.normalization: Hash sensitive identifiers, map framework classes coarsely, and align runtime records with scheduler/fabric windows.

range.notes: Detailed package/image/version values should be hashed or coarse-grained.

features.missingness_behavior: Runtime metadata may be absent under tenant isolation; absence weakens attribution but does not rule out training.
features.name: Rendezvous/rank mapping stability
features.normalization: Hash sensitive identifiers, map framework classes coarsely, and align runtime records with scheduler/fabric windows.

range.notes: 0 unstable or no mapping; 1 stable complete mapping over the window.

features.missingness_behavior: Runtime metadata may be absent under tenant isolation; absence weakens attribution but does not rule out training.
features.name: Container/VM image digest recurrence
features.normalization: Hash sensitive identifiers, map framework classes coarsely, and align runtime records with scheduler/fabric windows.

range.notes: Digest/hash identity should be privacy-preserving.

O10.name: Host, VM, container, process, and distributed-runtime metadata

features.missingness_behavior: Absent storage logs do not rule out cached/local training data; log delivery delay must be modeled separately.
features.name: Initial data staging volume
features.normalization: Aggregate by job/window/prefix class, hash sensitive paths, and distinguish staging, repeated reads, checkpoints, and export patterns.

range.notes: Absence does not rule out cached/local data.

features.missingness_behavior: Absent storage logs do not rule out cached/local training data; log delivery delay must be modeled separately.
features.name: Checkpoint write size
features.normalization: Aggregate by job/window/prefix class, hash sensitive paths, and distinguish staging, repeated reads, checkpoints, and export patterns.

range.notes: Periodic writes from many nodes can support training but backups can mimic.

features.missingness_behavior: Absent storage logs do not rule out cached/local training data; log delivery delay must be modeled separately.
features.name: Checkpoint period
features.normalization: Aggregate by job/window/prefix class, hash sensitive paths, and distinguish staging, repeated reads, checkpoints, and export patterns.

range.notes: 0 can mean no observed checkpoint period or instantaneous/undefined; define null-vs-zero carefully before modeling.

features.missingness_behavior: Absent storage logs do not rule out cached/local training data; log delivery delay must be modeled separately.
features.name: Read/write operation pattern
features.normalization: Aggregate by job/window/prefix class, hash sensitive paths, and distinguish staging, repeated reads, checkpoints, and export patterns.

range.notes: Includes read/write ratio

O11.name: Storage, object-store, filesystem, and data-movement logs

features.missingness_behavior: Missing ML logs are common and sensitive; absence is not no-training unless declarations/logging are mandatory and coverage is strong.
features.name: Declared model parameter count
features.normalization: Require authenticity, completeness, and alignment with scheduler/GPU/fabric windows before high-confidence semantic use.

range.notes: Excel suggests practical range 10^6-10^13+; use 0 for absent or none only if representation is explicit.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing ML logs are common and sensitive; absence is not no-training unless declarations/logging are mandatory and coverage is strong.
features.name: Training tokens / examples
features.normalization: Require authenticity, completeness, and alignment with scheduler/GPU/fabric windows before high-confidence semantic use.

range.notes: Excel suggests practical range 0-10^16+; boundary depends on policy definition.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing ML logs are common and sensitive; absence is not no-training unless declarations/logging are mandatory and coverage is strong.
features.name: Step count and step time
features.normalization: Require authenticity, completeness, and alignment with scheduler/GPU/fabric windows before high-confidence semantic use.

range.notes: Step count is non-negative; step time is non-negative; Excel practical step-count range is 1-10^7 when logs are present.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing ML logs are common and sensitive; absence is not no-training unless declarations/logging are mandatory and coverage is strong.
features.name: Loss/optimizer/checkpoint metadata
features.normalization: Require authenticity, completeness, and alignment with scheduler/GPU/fabric windows before high-confidence semantic use.

range.notes: Includes loss curves

O12.name: Workload declarations, experiment trackers, and ML training logs

features.missingness_behavior: Unknown or invalid attestation reduces trust; it is not positive training evidence by itself.
features.name: Attestation validity
features.normalization: Represent attestation as trust state/score and keep it separate from activity evidence.

range.notes: Score form 0-1; enum form has no numeric ordering unless explicitly mapped.

features.missingness_behavior: Unknown or invalid attestation reduces trust; it is not positive training evidence by itself.
features.name: Confidential-compute/security mode
features.normalization: Represent attestation as trust state/score and keep it separate from activity evidence.

range.notes: CC-On may disable performance counters; CC-DevTools may enable counters while weakening protections.

features.missingness_behavior: Unknown or invalid attestation reduces trust; it is not positive training evidence by itself.
features.name: Telemetry collector measurement
features.normalization: Represent attestation as trust state/score and keep it separate from activity evidence.

range.notes: Hash/signature values themselves are not ordered.

O13.name: Attestation, trusted computing, and device/telemetry provenance

features.missingness_behavior: This is the missingness layer; low coverage must cap no-run claims and can raise anomaly only when aligned with activity or integrity signals.
features.name: Telemetry coverage fraction by layer
features.normalization: Compute layer-specific coverage/gap fractions and a conservative minimum critical coverage for negative certification.

range.notes: Apply separately by layer; full coverage is needed for strong no-run claims.

features.missingness_behavior: This is the missingness layer; low coverage must cap no-run claims and can raise anomaly only when aligned with activity or integrity signals.
features.name: Telemetry gap fraction / missed scrapes
features.normalization: Compute layer-specific coverage/gap fractions and a conservative minimum critical coverage for negative certification.

range.notes: Count form is non-negative and unbounded over long windows; fraction form is 0-1.

features.missingness_behavior: This is the missingness layer; low coverage must cap no-run claims and can raise anomaly only when aligned with activity or integrity signals.
features.name: Clock drift / synchronization error
features.normalization: Compute layer-specific coverage/gap fractions and a conservative minimum critical coverage for negative certification.

range.notes: Larger drift undermines cross-layer correlation; use absolute drift.

features.missingness_behavior: This is the missingness layer; low coverage must cap no-run claims and can raise anomaly only when aligned with activity or integrity signals.
features.name: Counter reset/config change count
features.normalization: Compute layer-specific coverage/gap fractions and a conservative minimum critical coverage for negative certification.

range.notes: Counter resets can be normal or suspicious depending timing and explanation.

O14.name: Monitoring-pipeline integrity, coverage, and time synchronization

features.missingness_behavior: Missing physical logs weaken tamper analysis; physical events alone are not training evidence.
features.name: Rack-door/badge/maintenance events
features.normalization: Bucket and minimize physical-security data; align events with telemetry gaps, inventory deltas, and candidate windows.

range.notes: Privacy-sensitive; not training evidence by itself.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing physical logs weaken tamper analysis; physical events alone are not training evidence.
features.name: Firmware/BMC change events
features.normalization: Bucket and minimize physical-security data; align events with telemetry gaps, inventory deltas, and candidate windows.

range.notes: Approved changes can explain telemetry differences; unapproved changes are integrity-relevant.

O15.name: Physical security, maintenance, and change-management records

features.missingness_behavior: No probe result means no active-probe evidence; failed probes need benign-explanation analysis before evasion inference.
features.name: Probe throughput ratio
features.normalization: Compare observed probe result to randomized, hardware-calibrated expected result and account for power state and tenant-safety limits.

range.notes: Usually expected near 0-1+; denominator must be calibrated for hardware

features.missingness_behavior: No probe result means no active-probe evidence; failed probes need benign-explanation analysis before evasion inference.
features.name: Probe latency inflation
features.normalization: Compare observed probe result to randomized, hardware-calibrated expected result and account for power state and tenant-safety limits.

range.notes: High unexpected latency can indicate contention

features.missingness_behavior: No probe result means no active-probe evidence; failed probes need benign-explanation analysis before evasion inference.
features.name: VRAM residency / free-memory test
features.normalization: Compare observed probe result to randomized, hardware-calibrated expected result and account for power state and tenant-safety limits.

range.notes: Privacy-sensitive and potentially workload-disruptive.

O16.name: Active challenge probes and weak-trust telemetry tests

O17.category: Base capacity/discovery corroboration; weak activity signal

known_false_positives: leased/cloud capacity hidden from shipment data
power capacity not dedicated to AI
features.missingness_behavior: Absence of external evidence is weak; public coverage is incomplete and uncertain.
features.name: External IT power/capacity estimate
features.normalization: Track uncertainty explicitly and use external evidence for discovery/corroboration rather than active-run proof.

range.notes: External estimates can have large uncertainty and should not prove active training.

known_false_positives: leased/cloud capacity hidden from shipment data
power capacity not dedicated to AI
features.missingness_behavior: Absence of external evidence is weak; public coverage is incomplete and uncertain.
features.name: Construction/commissioning timeline
features.normalization: Track uncertainty explicitly and use external evidence for discovery/corroboration rather than active-run proof.

range.notes: Date range is calendar-dependent; use start/end/commissioning milestones rather than numeric min/max.

known_false_positives: leased/cloud capacity hidden from shipment data
power capacity not dedicated to AI
features.missingness_behavior: Absence of external evidence is weak; public coverage is incomplete and uncertain.
features.name: Chip shipment/procurement indicators
features.normalization: Track uncertainty explicitly and use external evidence for discovery/corroboration rather than active-run proof.

range.notes: Incomplete and often confidential; absence is weak evidence.

O17.name: External and out-of-band evidence

features.missingness_behavior: With strong scheduler coverage, zero allocation is strong absence for that layer; with weak coverage it is unknown, not zero activity.
features.name: Allocated GPU count per job or linked job group
features.normalization: Link jobs into policy-relevant groups, normalize accelerator count by hardware generation, and aggregate by topology domain/window.

range.notes: Derived from allocated GRES/TRES/device resources across a job or linked job group.

features.missingness_behavior: With strong scheduler coverage, zero allocation is strong absence for that layer; with weak coverage it is unknown, not zero activity.
features.name: Allocation duration
features.normalization: Link jobs into policy-relevant groups, normalize accelerator count by hardware generation, and aggregate by topology domain/window.

range.notes: Logical minimum is 0; upper bound is scheduler/accounting policy and retention dependent.

features.missingness_behavior: With strong scheduler coverage, zero allocation is strong absence for that layer; with weak coverage it is unknown, not zero activity.
features.name: GPU-hours / normalized compute-hours
features.normalization: Link jobs into policy-relevant groups, normalize accelerator count by hardware generation, and aggregate by topology domain/window.

range.notes: Usually allocated accelerator count multiplied by duration and hardware-normalization factor.

features.missingness_behavior: With strong scheduler coverage, zero allocation is strong absence for that layer; with weak coverage it is unknown, not zero activity.
features.name: Concurrency fraction of site/fabric
features.normalization: Link jobs into policy-relevant groups, normalize accelerator count by hardware generation, and aggregate by topology domain/window.

range.notes: Can exceed 1 only if denominator/scope is wrong or off-ledger capacity is omitted; such values should be discrepancy flags.

features.missingness_behavior: With strong scheduler coverage, zero allocation is strong absence for that layer; with weak coverage it is unknown, not zero activity.
features.name: Topology contiguity / allocation locality
features.normalization: Link jobs into policy-relevant groups, normalize accelerator count by hardware generation, and aggregate by topology domain/window.

range.notes: 0 scattered; 1 fully contiguous within the relevant low-latency topology.

features.missingness_behavior: With strong scheduler coverage, zero allocation is strong absence for that layer; with weak coverage it is unknown, not zero activity.
features.name: Declared workload class
features.normalization: Link jobs into policy-relevant groups, normalize accelerator count by hardware generation, and aggregate by topology domain/window.

range.notes: Treat as evidence, not proof.

features.missingness_behavior: With strong scheduler coverage, zero allocation is strong absence for that layer; with weak coverage it is unknown, not zero activity.
features.name: Reservation priority/preemption pattern
features.normalization: Link jobs into policy-relevant groups, normalize accelerator count by hardware generation, and aggregate by topology domain/window.

range.notes: Preemption/restart counts are non-negative; priority classes are scheduler-specific.

O2.name: Scheduler, job, reservation, and allocation metadata

features.missingness_behavior: Missing cloud-control data weakens attribution and provisioning claims; billing delay should be modeled separately from absence.
features.name: Batch provisioning event size
features.normalization: Map cloud instances/SKUs to accelerator counts and training-capable classes; aggregate by pseudonymous account/org and placement group.

range.notes: Upper bound depends on provider quota

features.missingness_behavior: Missing cloud-control data weakens attribution and provisioning claims; billing delay should be modeled separately from absence.
features.name: Capacity reservation/block duration
features.normalization: Map cloud instances/SKUs to accelerator counts and training-capable classes; aggregate by pseudonymous account/org and placement group.

range.notes: AWS Capacity Blocks expose provider-specific start/duration limits; other clouds differ.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing cloud-control data weakens attribution and provisioning claims; billing delay should be modeled separately from absence.
features.name: Training-instance-type fraction
features.normalization: Map cloud instances/SKUs to accelerator counts and training-capable classes; aggregate by pseudonymous account/org and placement group.

range.notes: Requires a versioned mapping of instance/SKU classes to training-capable accelerator classes.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing cloud-control data weakens attribution and provisioning claims; billing delay should be modeled separately from absence.
features.name: Billing continuity / diurnal index
features.normalization: Map cloud instances/SKUs to accelerator counts and training-capable classes; aggregate by pseudonymous account/org and placement group.

range.notes: 0 can represent perfectly flat/continuous usage under one definition; exact scale must be fixed before modeling.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing cloud-control data weakens attribution and provisioning claims; billing delay should be modeled separately from absence.
features.name: Egress / inter-region movement
features.normalization: Map cloud instances/SKUs to accelerator counts and training-capable classes; aggregate by pseudonymous account/org and placement group.

range.notes: Includes inter-region

O3.name: Cloud control-plane, capacity reservation, and billing records

features.missingness_behavior: Missing GPU counters are not zero activity; interpret through coverage, confidential-compute mode, and collector trust.
features.name: GPU utilization / busy percent
features.normalization: Aggregate per accelerator and job/domain using p50/p95/duty-cycle features; normalize power and bandwidth by configured cap/SKU.

range.notes: High utilization is activity evidence but not training semantics.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing GPU counters are not zero activity; interpret through coverage, confidential-compute mode, and collector trust.
features.name: SM active / Tensor Core activity
features.normalization: Aggregate per accelerator and job/domain using p50/p95/duty-cycle features; normalize power and bandwidth by configured cap/SKU.

range.notes: Availability varies by hardware

features.missingness_behavior: Missing GPU counters are not zero activity; interpret through coverage, confidential-compute mode, and collector trust.
features.name: HBM used fraction or bytes
features.normalization: Aggregate per accelerator and job/domain using p50/p95/duty-cycle features; normalize power and bandwidth by configured cap/SKU.

range.notes: Fraction form is 0-1; byte form is bounded by installed memory and partition/MIG profile.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing GPU counters are not zero activity; interpret through coverage, confidential-compute mode, and collector trust.
features.name: HBM/DRAM bandwidth active
features.normalization: Aggregate per accelerator and job/domain using p50/p95/duty-cycle features; normalize power and bandwidth by configured cap/SKU.

range.notes: Percent form is 0-100; GB/s form depends on accelerator SKU and measurement method.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing GPU counters are not zero activity; interpret through coverage, confidential-compute mode, and collector trust.
features.name: GPU power fraction of cap
features.normalization: Aggregate per accelerator and job/domain using p50/p95/duty-cycle features; normalize power and bandwidth by configured cap/SKU.

range.notes: Fraction form is 0-1; watt form is bounded by configured power cap/TDP.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing GPU counters are not zero activity; interpret through coverage, confidential-compute mode, and collector trust.
features.name: Per-process GPU memory/accounting
features.normalization: Aggregate per accelerator and job/domain using p50/p95/duty-cycle features; normalize power and bandwidth by configured cap/SKU.

range.notes: Process identities should be hashed/pseudonymized.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing GPU counters are not zero activity; interpret through coverage, confidential-compute mode, and collector trust.
features.name: XID/ECC/row-remap/PCIe replay errors
features.normalization: Aggregate per accelerator and job/domain using p50/p95/duty-cycle features; normalize power and bandwidth by configured cap/SKU.

range.notes: Health/reliability signal; not positive training evidence.

known_false_positives: inference kernels with high tensor throughput
features.missingness_behavior: Profiler unavailable is a coverage/trust fact, not evidence that no training occurred.
features.name: Kernel family / name hash sequence
features.normalization: Hash sensitive kernel names, group kernels into coarse classes, and compare throughput to hardware/model-specific expected peak.

range.notes: Values should be hashed or classed; no meaningful numeric min/max.

trust_requirements: profiler enabled or explicit reason unavailable

known_false_positives: inference kernels with high tensor throughput
features.missingness_behavior: Profiler unavailable is a coverage/trust fact, not evidence that no training occurred.
features.name: Achieved tensor throughput ratio
features.normalization: Hash sensitive kernel names, group kernels into coarse classes, and compare throughput to hardware/model-specific expected peak.

range.notes: Usually expected near 0-1 under a calibrated denominator; >1 means denominator or normalization needs review.

trust_requirements: profiler enabled or explicit reason unavailable

known_false_positives: inference kernels with high tensor throughput
features.missingness_behavior: Profiler unavailable is a coverage/trust fact, not evidence that no training occurred.
features.name: Profiler availability state
features.normalization: Hash sensitive kernel names, group kernels into coarse classes, and compare throughput to hardware/model-specific expected peak.

range.notes: Integrity/coverage feature, not activity evidence.

trust_requirements: profiler enabled or explicit reason unavailable

features.missingness_behavior: Missing local-fabric counters are common on some platforms; rely on coverage/trust rather than imputing zeros.
features.name: NVLink/NVSwitch link utilization
features.normalization: Aggregate by local fabric domain and correlate link bursts with GPU activity and runtime ranks.

range.notes: Measured per link/domain where counters are available.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing local-fabric counters are common on some platforms; rely on coverage/trust rather than imputing zeros.
features.name: NVLink/NVSwitch correlation/periodicity
features.normalization: Aggregate by local fabric domain and correlate link bursts with GPU activity and runtime ranks.

range.notes: Correlation component is -1 to 1; period component is non-negative seconds.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing local-fabric counters are common on some platforms; rely on coverage/trust rather than imputing zeros.
features.name: Link recovery/error events
features.normalization: Aggregate by local fabric domain and correlate link bursts with GPU activity and runtime ranks.

range.notes: Health signal; not training evidence.

O7.category: Primary activity / concurrency and synchronization signal

features.missingness_behavior: Low job-port mapping weakens attribution but not necessarily activity detection; missing fabric telemetry prevents strong no-run claims.
features.name: Scale-out port utilization
features.normalization: Aggregate by job/domain/fabric slice, normalize by line rate, and derive synchronized footprint and periodicity from correlated bursts.

range.notes: Applies to InfiniBand/RoCE/Ethernet/RDMA ports; thresholds are baseline-dependent.

features.missingness_behavior: Low job-port mapping weakens attribution but not necessarily activity detection; missing fabric telemetry prevents strong no-run claims.
features.name: Synchronized fabric footprint
features.normalization: Aggregate by job/domain/fabric slice, normalize by line rate, and derive synchronized footprint and periodicity from correlated bursts.

range.notes: Fraction form is 0-1; count form is bounded by monitored ports/nodes.

features.missingness_behavior: Low job-port mapping weakens attribution but not necessarily activity detection; missing fabric telemetry prevents strong no-run claims.
features.name: Collective periodicity / step cadence
features.normalization: Aggregate by job/domain/fabric slice, normalize by line rate, and derive synchronized footprint and periodicity from correlated bursts.

range.notes: Period is non-negative; regularity/score variants should be separately defined as 0-1.

features.missingness_behavior: Low job-port mapping weakens attribution but not necessarily activity detection; missing fabric telemetry prevents strong no-run claims.
features.name: RDMA congestion/XmitWait/retry/drops
features.normalization: Aggregate by job/domain/fabric slice, normalize by line rate, and derive synchronized footprint and periodicity from correlated bursts.

range.notes: Counter values are non-negative; percent-derived variants are 0-100.

features.missingness_behavior: Low job-port mapping weakens attribution but not necessarily activity detection; missing fabric telemetry prevents strong no-run claims.
features.name: Job-to-port mapping coverage
features.normalization: Aggregate by job/domain/fabric slice, normalize by line rate, and derive synchronized footprint and periodicity from correlated bursts.

range.notes: 0 none mapped; 1 all relevant flows mapped to job/pod/account.

O7.name: Scale-out network/fabric telemetry

O8.category: Secondary/primary corroboration; hard-to-fake physical activity signal

features.missingness_behavior: Missing power data undermines physical corroboration and no-run confidence; do not infer low power from missing meter data.
features.name: Rack IT power
features.normalization: Separate IT load from cooling overhead, normalize by rack/server design limit, and baseline-subtract non-accelerator load where possible.

range.notes: GB200-class racks can be around 120 kW; exact max is rack design dependent.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing power data undermines physical corroboration and no-run confidence; do not infer low power from missing meter data.
features.name: Facility IT power
features.normalization: Separate IT load from cooling overhead, normalize by rack/server design limit, and baseline-subtract non-accelerator load where possible.

range.notes: Use IT load separately from cooling overhead/PUE where possible.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing power data undermines physical corroboration and no-run confidence; do not infer low power from missing meter data.
features.name: Power continuity / coefficient of variation
features.normalization: Separate IT load from cooling overhead, normalize by rack/server design limit, and baseline-subtract non-accelerator load where possible.

range.notes: Lower variation often indicates steady sustained load; exact index definition must be fixed.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing power data undermines physical corroboration and no-run confidence; do not infer low power from missing meter data.
features.name: Power-to-telemetry consistency
features.normalization: Separate IT load from cooling overhead, normalize by rack/server design limit, and baseline-subtract non-accelerator load where possible.

range.notes: High power with low visible GPU/fabric telemetry should become a discrepancy flag

O8.name: Rack/server/facility power and energy telemetry

features.missingness_behavior: Missing thermal data weakens physical plausibility checks; thermal lag means absence of an immediate rise is not absence of activity.
features.name: GPU/HBM temperature
features.normalization: Compare thermal signals to ambient/cooling baseline and account for thermal lag relative to power and GPU activity.

range.notes: Hardware-specific thresholds; not universal.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing thermal data weakens physical plausibility checks; thermal lag means absence of an immediate rise is not absence of activity.
features.name: CDU or liquid-cooling delta-T
features.normalization: Compare thermal signals to ambient/cooling baseline and account for thermal lag relative to power and GPU activity.

range.notes: Thermal inertia can smooth short bursts.

features.missingness_behavior: Missing thermal data weakens physical plausibility checks; thermal lag means absence of an immediate rise is not absence of activity.
features.name: Cooling flow / fan speed
features.normalization: Compare thermal signals to ambient/cooling baseline and account for thermal lag relative to power and GPU activity.

range.notes: Percent form is 0-100; RPM/LPM form is hardware dependent.

aws_cur.supports: AWS Cost and Usage Reports for hourly/daily/monthly usage, costs, products, usage types, operations, and resource IDs where enabled.

aws_ec2_capacity_blocks.supports: Capacity Block reservation size, duration, start time, instance type, region, UltraCluster/UltraServer placement, and account limits.

aws_ec2_cloudtrail.supports: EC2 API event time, event name, request parameters, identity, source IP, region, and response elements.

aws_ec2_runinstances.supports: EC2 launch request fields including instance type, count, placement, networking, IAM, and capacity reservation targeting.

aws_efa.supports: Elastic Fabric Adapter as low-latency/high-throughput networking for HPC and ML workloads.

aws_s3_server_access_logging.supports: S3 request logging, object key formats, delivery timing, and best-effort completeness caveats.

epoch_frontier_datacenters.supports: External AI data-center capacity discovery via public disclosures, permits, imagery, and power estimates.

excel_workbook.note: Original working workbook used to derive this public catalogue; not required at runtime.

google_cloud_audit_logs.supports: Google Cloud audit log records for administrative and data access events.

google_gpu_monitoring.supports: GPU monitoring on Google Compute Engine VMs.

google_ops_agent_dcgm.supports: DCGM-based NVIDIA GPU metrics collected through Google Cloud Ops Agent.

kubernetes_device_plugins.supports: Kubernetes allocation/discovery of vendor devices such as GPUs through device plugins.

mlflow_tracking.supports: Experiment/run metadata, parameters, metrics, artifacts, model checkpoints, datasets, start/end times, and search.

nccl_env.supports: NCCL environment variables, debug logs, collectives, networking, topology, and communication configuration.

nvidia_dcgm_exporter.supports: DCGM/Prometheus GPU telemetry, SM/memory clocks, temperatures, power, energy, XID/errors, FB memory, NVLink bandwidth, and profiling counters.

nvidia_dgx_gb200_power.supports: DGX GB rack power shelf and rack-scale power context.

nvidia_dgx_h100_power.supports: DGX H100/H200 system power specification context.

nvidia_h100_confidential_computing.supports: H100 confidential computing modes, attestation, device certificates, and disabled performance counters in CC-On mode.

nvidia_ib_port_counters.supports: InfiniBand port counters for transmit/receive data, errors, congestion, retries, drops, and link health.

nvidia_smi.supports: NVIDIA GPU inventory, utilization, memory, power, temperature, clocks, processes, ECC, retired pages, row remap, MIG, and accounting fields.

nvidia_ufm_telemetry.supports: UFM telemetry for network bandwidth, congestion, errors, latency, XmitWait, and port-based monitoring.

pytorch_distributed.supports: PyTorch distributed process groups, collectives, backends, ranks, world size, and distributed APIs.

pytorch_distributed_checkpoint.supports: Distributed checkpoint APIs and checkpoint state handling.

pytorch_torchrun.supports: torchrun launch parameters, local rank, rank, world size, rendezvous, elasticity, and checkpointing expectations.

slurm_gres.supports: GPU generic resources, NVIDIA NVML autodetection, MIG support, MPS, and sharding configuration.

slurm_sacct.supports: Slurm job accounting fields, allocations, elapsed time, TRES usage, nodes, accounts, state, energy, and disk I/O.

slurm_tres.supports: Slurm trackable resources such as CPUs, memory, generic resources, energy, nodes, licenses, and filesystem metrics.

timing_memory_telemetry_gpu_governance.supports: Timing, memory, GEMM, proof-of-work-like, and VRAM-residency active probe concepts for weak-trust GPU utilization signals.

wandb_tracking.supports: Experiment tracking concepts, logged metrics, artifacts, runs, and model-development metadata.
